steps.title: Connect age to borrowing
steps.content: The hypothesis says a New York City Spanish dialect developed partly through interaction with English, the majority language. The research finding says younger nonmajority-language speakers borrow more from a region's majority language.

steps.title: Reject unsupported predictions
steps.content: Choice A adds unsupported neighborhood differences. Choice B predicts broader language and dialect growth. Choice C says younger speakers borrow more from nonmajority languages, which conflicts with the research finding. The answer is D.
